tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Use for discovery when you don't have a specific URL and need to find information, debug an error, research a topic, or track down an issue or news item. Returns an AI-synthesised answer plus a source list. By default the first few sources carry inline content (max_inline_sources, default 5); the rest are metadata-only. Drill into any source with web_fetch(url). The whole response is capped by a character budget; when truncated=true, trimmed sources carry a note telling you how to recover the full text via web_fetch or get_sources. Pass response_format=concise for answer plus source metadata only. If you already know the exact page URL, use web_fetch instead.

extra_sources.description: Optional supplemental source count. Tavily is primary; Firecrawl is fallback. If omitted, GROK_SEARCH_EXTRA_SOURCES is used.

recency_days.description: Restrict supplemental results to sources published within the last N days. Forwarded to Tavily as days+topic=news; also hinted to Grok prompt.

include_domains.description: Only return supplemental results from these domains. Tavily honors strictly; Grok receives as soft preference.

exclude_domains.description: Suppress supplemental results from these domains. Tavily honors strictly; Grok receives as soft instruction.

include_content.description: Inline source content via the resolve_content pipeline. Default true. Pass false to get summary plus source-list only. Superseded by response_format when both are set.

response_format.description: concise = synthesized answer plus source metadata only; detailed = inline source content, subject to the response budget. Takes precedence over include_content.

tools.name: get_sources
tools.description: Return cached sources from a previous web_search call by session_id. Use to re-examine sources already retrieved without issuing a new search. Paginate with offset/limit: the response reports total_sources and, when more pages remain, next_offset to pass as the next offset.

offset.description: Index of the first source to return. Use next_offset from the previous page to continue.

limit.description: Max sources in this page. Omit to return all remaining sources, still subject to the response budget.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Use when you already have a specific URL and want to read a single page in depth. GitHub issue/PR, StackOverflow (StackExchange), arXiv, and Wikipedia URLs are automatically parsed into structured, de-noised Markdown ready to feed an LLM; all other pages fall back to generic extraction. Returns {url, content, original_length, truncated, source_type, fallback_reason?}. If you do not have a URL yet and need to discover sources, use web_search instead.

max_chars.description: Optional character cap on returned content. Falls back to GROK_SEARCH_FETCH_MAX_CHARS, otherwise unlimited.

tools.name: wechat_search
tools.description: Search WeChat public-account articles via Sogou Weixin. Returns article metadata, resolved mp.weixin.qq.com URLs when available, and article content by default. Query is ordinary keyword recall; account/source filtering is applied locally with the account parameter rather than relying on unsupported field syntax.

query.description: Plain keyword query for Sogou Weixin recall. Boolean operators and field syntax are not guaranteed by the upstream.

account.description: Optional exact public-account/source filter, for example 机器之心, Datawhale, or 量子位.

pages.description: Sogou result pages to scan before local filtering and de-duplication.

include_content.description: Fetch and extract each resolved WeChat article body when possible. Failures are reported per article without failing the search.

max_content_chars.description: Optional per-article content cap. Defaults to GROK_SEARCH_FETCH_MAX_CHARS, then GROK_SEARCH_ENRICH_MAX_CHARS.

tools.name: zhihu_search
tools.description: Search Zhihu site content through Zhihu OpenAPI. Returns normalized metadata only: title, URL, author, summary, vote count, comment count, and edit time. Does not fetch full Zhihu page content.

query.description: Plain keyword query for Zhihu OpenAPI search.

count.description: Maximum number of Zhihu items to return.

tools.name: doctor
tools.description: Diagnostic probe: live connectivity check for Grok, Tavily, and Firecrawl backends, plus masked configuration. Use to verify the server is wired up and reachable.

verbose.description: Include detailed limits, logging status, provider wiring, and URL policy diagnostics.

tools.name: repo_metadata
tools.description: Fetch structured metadata for a GitHub repository or a Hugging Face model/dataset. Defaults to metadata only; pass include_readme for GitHub README text or include_card for Hugging Face model/dataset card text. This tool does not crawl commits, issues, files, releases, Spaces, or external links.

url.description: Repository URL. Supports https://github.com/{owner}/{repo}, https://huggingface.co/{repo_id}, and https://huggingface.co/datasets/{repo_id}.

provider.description: Provider to use when url is omitted.

repo_id.description: Full provider repo id when url is omitted, for example owner/repo or bert-base-uncased.

owner.description: Repository owner or Hugging Face namespace when using owner plus name input.

name.description: Repository name when using owner plus name input.

repo_type.description: Hugging Face repo type when url does not make it obvious. Defaults to model.

include_readme.description: For GitHub, fetch README text when available.

include_card.description: For Hugging Face, fetch model or dataset card text when available.

max_text_chars.description: Optional character cap for README/card text. Falls back to GROK_SEARCH_FETCH_MAX_CHARS.

tools.name: academic_search
tools.description: Search computer-science academic literature across dblp, Semantic Scholar, arXiv, OpenAlex, and Crossref. Results are deduplicated by DOI/arXiv/title and ranked with reciprocal rank fusion; OpenAlex/Crossref/Semantic metadata is used to enrich papers when available.

sources.description: Selected sources. Defaults depend on search_mode; balanced uses dblp, Semantic Scholar, and arXiv as primary sources and enriches from OpenAlex/Crossref when possible.

search_mode.description: balanced = stable CS discovery with metadata enrichment; broad = include all providers for maximum recall; precise = stricter title/query matching for known-paper lookups.

sort_by.description: Final ranking preference. relevance keeps query match first, citations boosts highly cited relevant papers, date boosts recent relevant papers. Provider APIs use matching native sort parameters when available.

extract_material_links.description: Attach detected code, dataset, model, demo, project, and documentation links from paper metadata without fetching those URLs.

tools.name: academic_get
tools.description: Resolve one academic paper by DOI, arXiv ID/URL, Semantic Scholar paperId, OpenAlex ID/URL, dblp URL/key, or title-like query. Returns normalized metadata and optionally citation/open-access enrichment.

extract_material_links.description: Attach detected code, dataset, model, demo, project, and documentation links from resolved paper metadata without fetching those URLs.

tools.name: academic_citations
tools.description: Return a summary of citing and referenced papers for one academic identifier. Uses Semantic Scholar first and OpenAlex as fallback; this is an overview, not a full citation graph crawl.

tools.name: academic_read
tools.description: Resolve and read an academic PDF as Markdown or text. Open-access sources are tried first: arXiv, Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, Unpaywall. Sci-Hub is only considered when explicitly enabled in configuration and remains the last fallback.

save_markdown_path.description: Write parsed Markdown/text to this file. Parent directories are created; existing files are rejected.

tools.name: academic_parse_pdf
tools.description: Resolve an academic PDF and return detailed parse metadata. Can save parsed Markdown to a requested path, prepare requested image/table output directories, report unsupported image/table extraction capabilities, and extract code/dataset/model/project links from metadata and parsed Markdown.

save_markdown_path.description: Write parsed Markdown/text to this file. Parent directories are created; existing files are rejected.

images_dir.description: Directory for future extracted images. Created when supplied; current engine reports unsupported for extraction.

tables_dir.description: Directory for future extracted tables. Created when supplied; current engine reports unsupported for extraction.

tools.name: academic_download_pdf
tools.description: Resolve and download an academic PDF to a local path without parsing it. Reuses the same full-text resolver, institutional access handling, proxy configuration, and PDF byte validation as academic_read/academic_parse_pdf. Existing files are rejected unless overwrite=true.

output_path.description: Target PDF file path. Parent directories are created.
